course_title: Strategic Management of Tourism and Events

learning_outcomes.description: Apply key concepts and processes from the strategic management field to tourism and events organisations.
Collaboratively evaluate strategies both at the micro (organisation) level and the macro (destination) level.
Undertake scenario planning in the tourism and events context to develop a future focus.
Apply an understanding of leadership theories, business practices and related ethics in appraising real world issues and events.
Reflect on your own skills and abilities as a future leader in the tourism and events industry.

assessments.due_date:
Week 3 Thu - Week 9 Thu

This assessment will take place during the scheduled tutorial times for this course.

Seven in-class tutorial activities (Weeks 3–9), students are required to complete a minimum of five (5) activities.

Students complete one activity each week during tutorials.

Students are expected to actively participate in tutorial discussions, workshops, data analysis activities and strategic exercises before completing their individual submission.
assessments.task_description:
Strategic managers in tourism and events are increasingly required to make decisions in complex, uncertain and rapidly changing environments. Effective strategic decision-making requires the ability to interpret evidence, analyse trends, evaluate competing stakeholder interests, anticipate future challenges, and develop appropriate responses. This assessment is designed to progressively develop these capabilities through a series of structured tutorial activities focused on Brisbane 2032 and the future of tourism and events.

This assessment consists of a sequence of seven (7) scaffolded in-class strategic analysis activities completed during Weeks 3–9 tutorials. Students are required to complete a minimum of five (5) activities.

Students may choose which activities to complete. Alternatively, students who complete more than five activities (each activity is worth 6%) will have their highest five marks counted towards the final Assessment 1 grade (30%).
assessments.submission_guidelines: • Submission for each activity is due by 4:00 pm on the same day as the tutorial in which the activity is undertaken.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.assessment_title: Assignment 2 Scenario Planning Presentation and Individual Defence
assessments.due_date:
Week 10 Thu - Week 11 Thu

This assessment will take place during the scheduled tutorial times for this course (Week 10 and Week 11)
assessments.task_description:
The Brisbane 2032 Olympic and Paralympic Games present both opportunities and challenges for tourism and events organisations. In this assessment, your group will apply scenario planning techniques to explore a plausible future Brisbane tourism environment in 2032 and develop strategic recommendations that will enable Brisbane to thrive under that future scenario. Following the presentation, each student will participate in an individual Q&A.

Group Presentation: Present the analysis and recommendations in class, with feedback provided by classmates in hypothetical roles as the senior management of the tourism or event organisation. Maximum 15 minutes per group. Peer Assessment Factor for this assessment: Conducted online, at the conclusion of all presentations (% fulfilment of individual role x actual mark awarded to group)

Individual Q&A: Following the presentation, each student will participate in an individual Q&A (Maximum 3-5 minutes per student). Students are expected to demonstrate individual understanding of the entire presentation.

Please Note: The presentation and Q&A will be recorded for marking purposes.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Late submissions are not accepted for in-class presentations. Failure to present at the scheduled time will result in a mark of zero.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Part A: Leadership Aspirations (Week 3) 14/08/2026 2:00 pm

Part B: Leadership Aspirations and Change (Week 13) 30/10/2026 2:00 pm
assessments.task_description: In Weeks 1 and 2, you will engage in a reflection on your current leadership aspirations within the tourism and events industry (Part A), considering questions such as: What personal and organisational goals will you pursue? This reflection forms the foundation for the summative Week 13 task (Part B), where you will reflect on your future as a leader in 2032, addressing four key areas: your leadership development over time, professional leadership experiences, application of course concepts, and how you anticipate and prepare for future industry challenges.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
